VOLLAND SPX GREEK HEDGING

GREEK HEDGING (SPX) ESTIMATES THE DIRECTION AND SIZE OF DAILY DEALER REBALANCING FLOWS IMPLIED BY THE OPTIONS MARKET.

DELTA HEDGING (~$35.66B): SUGGESTS DEALERS MAY NEED TO BUY UNDERLYING (OR FUTURES) TO STAY HEDGED AGAINST PRICE MOVES, IMPLYING POTENTIALLY STRONG FLOW IMPACT.
VEGA HEDGING (~-$8.41B): EXPOSURE TO CHANGES IN IMPLIED VOLATILITY; TYPICALLY MANAGED THROUGH REPRICING OPTIONS, WITH THE NEGATIVE FIGURE INDICATING DECLINING VOLATILITY SENSITIVITY.
THETA HEDGING (~$15.60M): THE IMPACT OF ONE DAY PASSING ON THE DEALER BOOK; LIKE VEGA, THIS IS LARGELY HANDLED THROUGH OPTION REPRICING, WITH TIME DECAY ADDING MODESTLY TO HEDGING NEEDS.
GREEK HEDGING: NET NOTIONAL DEALER HEDGING THAT NEEDS TO BE APPLIED BY THE END OF THE DAY
